meaning_en: To leave something or someone permanently
example: They had to abandon the plan due to lack of funds.
mnemonic: A BAND ON a sinking ship — everyone abandons it.

meaning_en: Not present at a place or event
example: Three students were absent from class today.
mnemonic: ABSENT — AB + SENT; sent away from the place.

meaning_en: To take in liquid, knowledge, or impact
example: A sponge absorbs water quickly.
mnemonic: ABSORB — AB + SORB; soak it all in like a sorbet absorbs flavour.

meaning_en: Correct in every detail; exact
example: The weather forecast was accurate — it rained at noon.
mnemonic: ACCURATE — A + CURE + ATE; a cure that is precisely dosed is accurate.

meaning_en: To accept or admit the existence of something
example: She acknowledged her mistake in front of the class.
mnemonic: ACKNOWLEDGE — ACK + KNOWLEDGE; use your KNOWLEDGE to ack(nowledge).

meaning_en: To change to suit new conditions
example: Animals adapt to their environments over time.
mnemonic: ADAPT — AD + APT; become APT (suitable) for a new situation.

meaning_en: Enough for what is needed
example: The food supply was adequate for the whole week.
mnemonic: ADEQUATE — ADE + QUATE; adequately equal to the task.

meaning_en: To make small changes to improve something
example: She adjusted the mirror before driving.
mnemonic: ADJUST — AD + JUST; make it JUST right by adjusting.

meaning_en: To have enough money or resources for something
example: We can't afford to make any more mistakes.
mnemonic: AFFORD — A FORD; you can afford a Ford but not a Ferrari.

meaning_en: The science and practice of farming
example: India's economy depends heavily on agriculture.
mnemonic: AGRICULTURE — AGRI + CULTURE; cultivating the land = agriculture.

meaning_en: Help or support given to those in need
example: The government sent aid to the flood-affected regions.
mnemonic: AID — RAID; an aid worker arrives before a disaster raids a village.

meaning_en: To cause someone to feel worried or frightened
example: The sudden noise alarmed everyone in the room.
mnemonic: ALARM — A + LARM; an alarm raises your ARM in fear.

meaning_en: A vehicle that takes sick or injured people to hospital
example: Call an ambulance — someone has been injured.
mnemonic: AMBULANCE — AMBU + LANCE; lances (moves) fast to help the sick.

meaning_en: A detailed examination of something to understand it
example: The analysis of the data revealed surprising results.
mnemonic: ANALYSIS — ANAL + Y + SIS; break it apart to analyse it.

meaning_en: A feeling of worry or nervousness about uncertain events
example: She felt great anxiety before her first public speech.
mnemonic: ANXIETY — ANXIOUS + TY; the state of feeling anxious.

meaning_en: To say sorry for something you have done wrong
example: He apologised sincerely for his behaviour.
mnemonic: APOLOGISE — APOLOGY + ISE; making an apology.

meaning_en: To make a serious request; to be attractive to someone
example: She appealed to the court to review the decision.
mnemonic: APPEAL — A + PEEL; peel back the layers to find what appeals to you.

meaning_en: To come near; a method or way of doing something
example: We need a different approach to solve this problem.
mnemonic: APPROACH — A + PROACH; reach closer = approach.

meaning_en: Made by humans, not natural
example: Artificial intelligence is changing the world rapidly.
mnemonic: ARTIFICIAL — ARTIST + ICIAL; what an artist makes artificially.

meaning_en: Feeling embarrassed or guilty about something
example: She was ashamed of her rude behaviour.
mnemonic: ASHAMED — ASH + AMED; burnt to ASH with shame.

meaning_en: To evaluate or judge the quality or value of something
example: Teachers assess students through exams and projects.
mnemonic: ASSESS — ASS + ESS; assess the essential aspects.

meaning_en: To believe something is true without proof
example: Don't assume — always verify the facts.
mnemonic: ASSUME — ASS + U + ME; ASSuming makes an ASS of U and ME.

meaning_en: To try to do something
example: She made several attempts before passing the driving test.
mnemonic: ATTEMPT — AT + TEMPT; tempted to try = attempt.

meaning_en: Knowledge or understanding of a situation
example: Public awareness of mental health is growing.
mnemonic: AWARENESS — AWARE + NESS; the state of being aware.

meaning_en: An item bought cheaply; to negotiate a price
example: She got a great bargain at the sale.
mnemonic: BARGAIN — BAR + GAIN; gain at the bar (market) = bargain.

meaning_en: Something that blocks progress or prevents access
example: Language can be a barrier to communication.
mnemonic: BARRIER — BAR + RIER; a BAR blocks the way = barrier.

meaning_en: The underlying reason or foundation for something
example: Decisions should be made on the basis of facts.
mnemonic: BASIS — BASE + IS; the BASE IS the basis.

meaning_en: To hold someone responsible for something bad
example: Don't blame others for your own mistakes.
mnemonic: BLAME — FLAME; you FLAME (accuse) someone when you BLAME them.

meaning_en: To increase or improve something
example: A good breakfast boosts your energy for the day.
mnemonic: BOOST — ROOST; a bird BOOSTs off its ROOST with energy.

meaning_en: To send out information on TV or radio; to spread widely
example: The match was broadcast live on national television.
mnemonic: BROADCAST — BROAD + CAST; casting information BROADLY.

meaning_en: A plan for how money will be spent
example: The government presented the annual budget in parliament.
mnemonic: BUDGET — BUD + GET; like a BUD, a budget helps money GROW wisely.

meaning_en: A serious disease caused by uncontrolled cell growth
example: Early detection is key to treating cancer successfully.
mnemonic: CANCER — CAN + CER; a disease that CAN be treated with CARE.

meaning_en: Having the ability or skill to do something
example: She is capable of managing a team of fifty people.
mnemonic: CAPABLE — CAP + ABLE; wearing the right CAP means you're ABLE = capable.

meaning_en: To catch and hold someone or something
example: The photographer captured a beautiful sunset.
mnemonic: CAPTURE — CAP + TURE; put a CAP on it to capture it.

meaning_en: A sudden disaster causing great damage
example: The oil spill was an environmental catastrophe.
mnemonic: CATASTROPHE — CAT + A + STROPHE; a CAT tangled in a strophe (twist) = chaos.

meaning_en: A person who legally belongs to a country
example: Every citizen has the right to vote.
mnemonic: CITIZEN — CITY + ZEN; a ZEN person of the CITY = citizen.

meaning_en: To fall down suddenly; to fail completely
example: The old bridge collapsed during the storm.
mnemonic: COLLAPSE — COL + LAPSE; a lapse in structure causes collapse.

meaning_en: Dedication to something; a promise to do something
example: Success requires commitment and daily effort.
mnemonic: COMMITMENT — COMMIT + MENT; the mental state of being committed.

meaning_en: An abstract idea or general understanding
example: The concept of democracy is based on equality.
mnemonic: CONCEPT — CON + CEPT; conceiving (cept) an idea = concept.

meaning_en: Worry about something; to be about something important
example: Environmental pollution is a major concern worldwide.
mnemonic: CONCERN — CON + CERN; a CONCERN is a matter that con(cerns) the mind.

meaning_en: The end or final decision of something
example: The report reached the conclusion that more funding is needed.
mnemonic: CONCLUSION — CON + CLUSION; closing (clusion) everything = conclusion.

meaning_en: A serious disagreement or fight
example: The conflict between the two countries lasted for years.
mnemonic: CONFLICT — CON + FLICT; two forces flick (flict) against each other.

meaning_en: A result or effect of an action
example: Every action has a consequence — good or bad.
mnemonic: CONSEQUENCE — CON + SEQUENCE; a sequence (chain) of results = consequences.

meaning_en: The protection of nature and the environment
example: Wildlife conservation is essential for biodiversity.
mnemonic: CONSERVATION — CONSERVE + ATION; the act of conserving nature.

meaning_en: The circumstances that form the background of an event
example: Understand the context before drawing conclusions.
mnemonic: CONTEXT — CON + TEXT; the TEXT around a word gives its context.

meaning_en: To give something to help a shared goal
example: Everyone should contribute to keeping the city clean.
mnemonic: CONTRIBUTE — CON + TRIBUTE; pay TRIBUTE together = contribute.

meaning_en: Causing disagreement or strong opinions
example: The new law was highly controversial.
mnemonic: CONTROVERSIAL — CONTRO + VERSE + Y; verses (arguments) controvert each other.

meaning_en: To deal with a difficult situation successfully
example: It was hard to cope with the loss of her pet.
mnemonic: COPE — HOPE; you need HOPE to COPE.

meaning_en: The ability to face danger or difficulty without fear
example: It takes courage to speak the truth in difficult times.
mnemonic: COURAGE — CORE + AGE; at the CORE of every age is courage.

meaning_en: A time of intense difficulty or danger
example: The country is going through an economic crisis.
mnemonic: CRISIS — CRY + SIS; when a crisis hits, everyone cries.

meaning_en: Extremely important and necessary
example: It is crucial to stay hydrated during exercise.
mnemonic: CRUCIAL — CRUX + AL; the CRUX (core) of the matter = crucial.

meaning_en: A remedy that restores health; to make someone healthy again
example: Scientists are working on a cure for the disease.
mnemonic: CURE — PURE; a PURE medicine can CURE.

meaning_en: Facts and statistics collected for analysis
example: The research was based on data collected from 1,000 people.
mnemonic: DATA — DATE + A; data from past DATES tells us about patterns.

meaning_en: The latest time by which something must be completed
example: The assignment deadline is Friday at 5 PM.
mnemonic: DEADLINE — DEAD + LINE; cross the line or it's DEAD (over).

meaning_en: A formal discussion with opposing viewpoints
example: The students held a debate on climate change.
mnemonic: DEBATE — DE + BATE; bait (bate) both sides into arguing = debate.

meaning_en: To decrease in quality or quantity; to refuse politely
example: The population of tigers has declined drastically.
mnemonic: DECLINE — DE + CLINE; cline (slope) going downward = decline.

meaning_en: To win against someone; the state of being beaten
example: The team suffered a heavy defeat in the final.
mnemonic: DEFEAT — DE + FEAT; your great FEAT is gone when defeated.

meaning_en: Protection from attack; justification for one's actions
example: The country spent billions on national defence.
mnemonic: DEFENCE — DE + FENCE; a FENCE protects = defence.

meaning_en: To show or prove something clearly
example: She demonstrated how to use the new software.
mnemonic: DEMONSTRATE — DEMON + STRATE; a demon STRATEgy made visible = demonstrate.

meaning_en: A mental illness causing persistent sadness; an economic downturn
example: Many young people suffer from depression and anxiety.
mnemonic: DEPRESSION — DE + PRESS + ION; pressing down on your emotions = depression.

meaning_en: To be worthy of a reward or punishment
example: You deserve a rest after all that hard work.
mnemonic: DESERVE — DE + SERVE; what you SERVE (do) is what you DESERVE back.

meaning_en: Firmness of purpose; the process of deciding something
example: Her determination to succeed inspired everyone.
mnemonic: DETERMINATION — DETERMINE + ATION; being determined = determination.

meaning_en: A physical or mental condition that limits a person's abilities
example: Schools should be accessible to students with disabilities.
mnemonic: DISABILITY — DIS + ABILITY; when ability is DISrupted.

meaning_en: Training to follow rules; self-control
example: Academic success requires discipline and focus.
mnemonic: DISCIPLINE — DISCIPLE + N; a DISCIPLE follows discipline.

meaning_en: A range of different things, people, or ideas
example: India is celebrated for its cultural diversity.
mnemonic: DIVERSITY — DIVERSE + ITY; the state of being diverse.

meaning_en: Relating to the home or to one's own country
example: Domestic flights are cheaper than international ones.
mnemonic: DOMESTIC — DOME + STIC; inside the DOME (home) = domestic.

meaning_en: Most important, powerful, or influential
example: English is the dominant language in global business.
mnemonic: DOMINANT — DOM + INANT; the DOM(ain) rules — dominant.

meaning_en: A long period of unusually low rainfall
example: The drought destroyed the crops for two years.
mnemonic: DROUGHT — DRY + OUGHT; the land OUGHT to be DRY in a drought.

meaning_en: Doing something well with minimum waste of time or energy
example: Solar panels are an efficient way to produce energy.
mnemonic: EFFICIENT — EFFECT + IENT; producing the best EFFECT with less effort.

meaning_en: The process of choosing leaders by voting
example: The general election will be held next month.
mnemonic: ELECTION — ELECT + ION; people ELECT through an election.

meaning_en: To completely remove or get rid of something
example: We must eliminate plastic waste from our oceans.
mnemonic: ELIMINATE — E + LIMINATE; remove the LIMITS = eliminate.

meaning_en: To come out from somewhere; to become known
example: A new leader emerged after the elections.
mnemonic: EMERGE — E + MERGE; merge out of darkness and emerge.

meaning_en: Special importance or stress given to something
example: The teacher placed emphasis on correct pronunciation.
mnemonic: EMPHASIS — EM + PHASE + IS; the phase that is most important.

meaning_en: To make something possible or give the ability to do it
example: Technology enables people to work from home.
mnemonic: ENABLE — EN + ABLE; make someone ABLE = enable.

meaning_en: Intense enjoyment and eagerness for something
example: She approached the new project with great enthusiasm.
mnemonic: ENTHUSIASM — EN + THUSE + IASM; fill with THUSE (heat of passion).

meaning_en: To set up or create something on a firm basis
example: The company was established in 1998.
mnemonic: ESTABLISH — ESTATE + LISH; building an ESTATE = establishing.

meaning_en: Facts or information indicating whether something is true
example: The police found strong evidence at the crime scene.
mnemonic: EVIDENCE — EVIDENT + CE; what is evident (clear) becomes evidence.

meaning_en: The gradual development of something over time
example: Darwin's theory of evolution changed science forever.
mnemonic: EVOLUTION — EVOLVE + TION; the process of evolving.

meaning_en: To increase in size, number, or importance
example: The company plans to expand into new markets.
mnemonic: EXPAND — EX + PAND; like a PANDA spreading out — expand.

meaning_en: A belief that something will happen
example: She exceeded everyone's expectations.
mnemonic: EXPECTATION — EXPECT + ATION; what you expect = expectation.

meaning_en: A high level of skill or knowledge in a field
example: The project required expertise in engineering.
mnemonic: EXPERTISE — EXPERT + ISE; having EXPERT knowledge = expertise.

meaning_en: To examine or investigate an unfamiliar area
example: They explored the ancient ruins in the forest.
mnemonic: EXPLORE — EX + PLORE; go beyond the shore (plore) to explore.

meaning_en: Lack of success; someone who does not succeed
example: Failure is a stepping stone to success.
mnemonic: FAILURE — FAIL + URE; the result of failing.

meaning_en: Complete trust; a strong religious belief
example: She had faith that things would improve.
mnemonic: FAITH — WAIT; have FAITH and WAIT — things will come.

meaning_en: The state of being known by many people
example: He achieved fame as a musician at a young age.
mnemonic: FAME — FLAME; fame burns bright like a flame.

meaning_en: Information about reactions to something, used to improve it
example: The teacher gave useful feedback on the essay.
mnemonic: FEEDBACK — FEED + BACK; feed the information BACK to improve.

meaning_en: Able to change or be adapted easily
example: A flexible schedule helps manage work and life.
mnemonic: FLEXIBLE — FLEX + IBLE; flex your muscles = flexible.

meaning_en: To officially not allow something
example: Smoking is forbidden inside the building.
mnemonic: FORBID — FOR + BID; your bid (offer) is rejected = forbidden.

meaning_en: The base on which something is built; an organisation
example: A strong foundation is key to any successful building.
mnemonic: FOUNDATION — FOUND + ATION; what is FOUND at the base.

meaning_en: The power to act, speak, or think as one wishes
example: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right.
mnemonic: FREEDOM — FREE + DOM; a FREE DOMAIN = freedom.

meaning_en: To cause someone to feel annoyed or discouraged
example: Repeated failures frustrated her deeply.
mnemonic: FRUSTRATE — FRUST + RATE; when things FRUST-rate your progress.

meaning_en: Forming the most essential or important part of something
example: Clean water is a fundamental human right.
mnemonic: FUNDAMENTAL — FUND + AMENTAL; a fund is the FUNDAMENT(al) base of finance.

meaning_en: All the people born around the same time
example: Each generation faces its own unique challenges.
mnemonic: GENERATION — GENERATE + ION; what a generation generates for the future.

meaning_en: Truly what it appears to be; sincere
example: Her smile was genuine — not forced.
mnemonic: GENUINE — GENE + WINE; genuine like original DNA (gene), not fake.

meaning_en: Relating to the whole world
example: Global warming affects every country on earth.
mnemonic: GLOBAL — GLOBE + AL; relating to the whole globe.

meaning_en: Slowly, over a period of time
example: Her health improved gradually after treatment.
mnemonic: GRADUALLY — GRADE + UALLY; moving up GRADES slowly = gradually.

meaning_en: A promise that something will happen or be done
example: The company offers a two-year guarantee on all products.
mnemonic: GUARANTEE — GUARD + ANTEE; a GUARD who ANTEEds (ensures) = guarantee.

meaning_en: The natural environment where a plant or animal lives
example: Deforestation destroys the natural habitat of many animals.
mnemonic: HABITAT — HABIT + AT; where a creature has its HABIT AT.

meaning_en: The prevention and treatment of illness and disease
example: Access to affordable healthcare is a human right.
mnemonic: HEALTHCARE — HEALTH + CARE; caring for HEALTH = healthcare.

meaning_en: To pause before doing something due to uncertainty
example: Don't hesitate to ask questions in class.
mnemonic: HESITATE — HESI + TATE; the Tate (state) of HESITATION.

meaning_en: Who or what someone is; the qualities that define a person
example: Culture plays a big role in shaping our identity.
mnemonic: IDENTITY — IDENT + ITY; what IDENTIFIES you = identity.

meaning_en: The process of coming to live permanently in another country
example: Immigration laws vary greatly between countries.
mnemonic: IMMIGRATION — IMMIGRATE + ION; people who immigrate = immigration.

meaning_en: A strong effect or influence
example: The pandemic had a huge impact on the economy.
mnemonic: IMPACT — IM + PACT; a PACT that makes a strong impression.

meaning_en: Money received from work or investments
example: A good education leads to a higher income.
mnemonic: INCOME — IN + COME; money that COMES IN = income.

meaning_en: More and more over time
example: Smartphones are increasingly being used in classrooms.
mnemonic: INCREASINGLY — INCREASING + LY; in an increasing manner.

meaning_en: Freedom from control; the state of not depending on others
example: India achieved independence on 15th August 1947.
mnemonic: INDEPENDENCE — IN + DEPEND + ENCE; the state of not depending.

meaning_en: A new idea, method, or invention
example: Technology innovation is happening faster than ever.
mnemonic: INNOVATION — INNOV + ATION; something NEW (novo = new) = innovation.

meaning_en: To make someone feel motivated or creative
example: Her story inspired thousands of young students.
mnemonic: INSPIRE — IN + SPIRE; breathe (spire) life and energy IN = inspire.

meaning_en: The ability to learn and understand quickly
example: Artificial intelligence is transforming industries.
mnemonic: INTELLIGENCE — INTEL + LIGENCE; Intel chips = fast intelligence.

meaning_en: Money spent to earn a profit in the future
example: Education is the best investment you can make.
mnemonic: INVESTMENT — INVEST + MENT; investing your mental energy.

meaning_en: An important topic or problem; to officially provide something
example: The government issued a new policy on education.
mnemonic: ISSUE — IS + SUE; is it an issue? Someone will sue!

meaning_en: Fair treatment or behaviour; the law and legal system
example: The victims demanded justice for what they suffered.
mnemonic: JUSTICE — JUST + ICE; cold hard JUST+ICE = justice.

meaning_en: The visible features of an area of land; a wide view
example: The mountain landscape was breathtaking.
mnemonic: LANDSCAPE — LAND + SCAPE; the SCAPE (view) of the LAND.

meaning_en: The action of leading a group or organisation
example: Strong leadership is essential in times of crisis.
mnemonic: LEADERSHIP — LEADER + SHIP; a LEADER steers the SHIP.

meaning_en: Money borrowed that must be repaid
example: She took a student loan to pay for her education.
mnemonic: LOAN — MOAN; you MOAN when you have to repay a LOAN.

meaning_en: Faithful and devoted to someone or something
example: A loyal friend supports you even in hard times.
mnemonic: LOYAL — ROY + AL; a ROYAL servant is always LOYAL.

meaning_en: More than half of a total group
example: The majority of students passed the exam.
mnemonic: MAJORITY — MAJOR + ITY; the MAJOR (bigger) part = majority.

meaning_en: Very large in size or amount
example: There was a massive crowd at the concert.
mnemonic: MASSIVE — MASS + IVE; a huge MASS = massive.

meaning_en: A metal disc awarded for achievement in sport or bravery
example: She won a gold medal at the Olympics.
mnemonic: MEDAL — METAL; a MEDAL is a METAL reward.

meaning_en: Relating to the armed forces or soldiers
example: The military was deployed to assist in the disaster area.
mnemonic: MILITARY — MILI + TARY; a tary (territory) defended by mili(tia).
